title: Johnson & Johnson — Projects
html:
Module 5: Active Projects & Workstream Intelligence

Governing Thought

The J&J account has 14 active workstreams generating regular deliverables across Oncology, Immunology, Neuroscience, and cross-brand programs. The account is in growth mode: MEE (MSL Effectiveness Evaluation) expansion across all TAs kicked off in Q1 2026, a TREMFYA global proposal worth $568K is in motion, and established trackers are performing consistently. Three performance themes cut across nearly every workstream: (1) J&J leads in market presence and SOV across most workstreams, giving the field force a structural advantage; (2) execution gaps persist in clinical depth, access discussions, and problem-solving attributes, meaning presence alone is not converting to maximum impact; and (3) emerging competitor momentum — Nubeqa in prostate, Vraylar in neuroscience, AbbVie in immunology — is narrowing or erasing J&J's leads in specific segments. A Principal's job is to connect these dots across workstreams so J&J stakeholders see the system-level story, not just brand-by-brand scorecards.

Account Health Dashboard

Workstream Status Last Deliverable Key Finding
ERLEADA PET — ONCs :yellow_circle: Watch Q4 2025 ES SOV softened; call quality and Rx intent declined among RWE Targets
ERLEADA PET — UROs :green_circle: Strong Q4 2025 ES Interactions more impactful QoQ; led competitors among RWE Targets
GU MSL :yellow_circle: Watch Q4 2025 ES Dominant SOV but trails on clinical education clarity
GASTRO MSL :green_circle: Strong Q4 2025 ES 88% reach, 36% SOV; slight lead on call quality
SPRAVATO PET :red_circle: Risk Q4 2025 ES LTIP and perception steep decline; Utility Players driving erosion
IMM FRM (TREMFYA) :yellow_circle: Watch Q4 2025 ES Strong PSP intent but ~55-60% report no motivation to act post-interaction
KAM (Onc Cross-Brand) :green_circle: Strong Q4 2025 ES #1 partner of choice; extended KAM reach lead
IMM KAM-HS :green_circle: Strong Q4 2025 ES Leading reach, frequency, SOV; strong visual aid use
IMM ABS :yellow_circle: Watch Q4 2025 ES Co-leads SOV but performance only at par
IMM KAM-MD :green_circle: Strong Q4 2025 ES 50% SOV; ~95% stakeholder coverage
MM OCE (Heme) :green_circle: Strong H2 2025 ES Maintained SOV leadership; DARZALEX advantage over Sarclisa
MM Bispecifics (PHDM) :yellow_circle: Watch Q4 2025 ES Cost is top challenge; Elrexfio improving on impact metrics
RYBREVANT Social Listening :green_circle: Active Q4 2025 ES Growing online discussion; misconceptions around mutation eligibility
MEE Tracking (All TAs) :blue_circle: New Skeleton deck (Mar 2026) Q1 2026 kick-offs complete; currently in recruitment
BD / Growth :green_circle: Active TREMFYA GI Global Proposal (Mar 2026) $568K proposal submitted; 2026 BD resources documented

Legend: :green_circle: Strong/On Track | :yellow_circle: Watch (declining metric or execution gap) | :red_circle: Risk (significant decline requiring intervention) | :blue_circle: New (recently launched)

Detailed Workstream Profiles

Oncology PET

ERLEADA PET (Prostate — ONCs)

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • Successful interactions remained stable, leading Xtandi and closely trailing Nubeqa
  • New dispersible tablet and bioavailability messages tested high-impact when recalled

Watch items:

  • SOV softened QoQ despite stable execution; Nubeqa SOV rose to all-time high — competitive pressure intensifying
  • ERLEADA interactions less impactful in Q4; call quality and Rx intent declined among RWE Targets, especially on clinical knowledge

Recs from latest readout:

  • Focus on RWE data discussions and comparative efficacy/safety to regain ground with RWE Targets
  • Address tolerability concerns proactively; leverage visual aids to boost new message recall

SharePoint: [link]

ERLEADA PET (Prostate — UROs)

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • ERLEADA interactions more impactful QoQ among RWE Targets, leading all competitors
  • Rep performance improved notably across most attributes; closing improved substantially for RWE Targets

Watch items:

  • SOV softened — likely linked to increased INLEXZO launch focus pulling share of time
  • Access question performance remains a gap; low FRM follow-up rate on access issues

Recs from latest readout:

  • Elevate rep perceptions on access questions via coordinated FRM follow-ups
  • Leverage visual aids to reinforce dosing messages; build on OS RWE vs AA message momentum

SharePoint: [link]

SPRAVATO PET (Neuroscience)

Status: Active — At Risk | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • SPRAVATO excels in delivering new information, particularly on insurance/access and efficacy
  • Best message combination identified: Efficacy 1-day + Affordability

Watch items:

  • LTIP and product perception saw steep decline in Q4 2025; Utility Players (non-specialist reps) are the primary driver of this erosion
  • SOV trails key competitors; Vraylar leads. 60%+ HCPs have newer relationships with SPRAVATO reps vs more established competitor reps

Recs from latest readout:

  • Prioritize consistent rep assignment to build relationship tenure; tailor approach by persona segment
  • Pair Efficacy 1-day with Affordability messaging; prioritize new data delivery

SharePoint: [link]

Oncology MSL / FRM / KAM

GU MSL (Prostate + Bladder)

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • J&J MSLs dominate GU engagement: highest SOV and reach vs all competitors
  • Bladder (INLEXZO): J&J MSLs lead competitors on most quality attributes; sustained drug delivery and durable CR are the most recalled narratives

Watch items:

  • Prostate: J&J MSLs on par for quality but trail competitors on enhancing understanding and clear clinical education — a depth-of-engagement gap
  • OS RWE perceived less believable among prostate oncologists; bladder-preservation narrative highly valued but lags in recall and believability

Recs from latest readout:

  • Anchor ERLEADA prostate discussions in real-world patient cases + OS RWE; use visual aids to improve believability
  • Continue INLEXZO sustained-delivery narrative; elevate bladder-preservation story with supporting evidence

SharePoint: [link]

KAM (Oncology Cross-Brand)

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • J&J is #1 partner of choice among key accounts; KAM quality is the leading driver of that ranking
  • Extended lead in KAM reach among key accounts; slightly led competitors on driving follow-up actions (5 vs 4-4.5)

Watch items:

  • Less impactful in driving PHDMs to reassess contracts — informational visits outweighing decision-driving actions
  • Reimbursement discussion frequency decreased QoQ

Recs from latest readout:

  • Increase community-based PHDM engagement; prioritize decision-driving actions over purely informational visits
  • Strengthen economic and reimbursement discussions to close the contract-reassessment gap

SharePoint: [link]

MM OCE (Heme)

Status: Active | Latest: H2 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: H2 2025

What's working:

  • J&J maintained SOV leadership in H2 2025; DARZALEX maintained advantage vs Sarclisa
  • TECVAYLI and TALVEY demonstrated competitive OCE performance; led in likelihood to advocate vs Elrexfio

Watch items:

  • Softer perceptions on DARZALEX clinical profile knowledge in group interactions and among non-APPs
  • TECVAYLI dosing schedule message recall declined in Q4 2025

Recs from latest readout:

  • Reinforce high-impact messaging across the MM portfolio, especially dosing schedule for TECVAYLI
  • Address unmet needs on PSP, adherence, and QoL across all MM brands

SharePoint: [link]

MM Bispecifics (PHDM View)

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • TECVAYLI and TALVEY capture leading SOV in the MM bispecifics space
  • ~40% of PHDMs report no educational gaps for TALVEY — strong knowledge saturation

Watch items:

  • Cost to patient and institution are the top challenges for TECVAYLI — an access/economic barrier
  • Elrexfio interactions led to notable improvements across impact metrics, signaling competitive momentum

Recs from latest readout:

  • Address PSP and safety needs for TECVAYLI; efficacy and safety for Lynozyfic
  • Monitor Elrexfio's impact trajectory — improvement trend could erode TECVAYLI's advocacy lead

SharePoint: [link]

Immunology

IMM FRM (TREMFYA)

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary (SWOT format) | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • Leading SOV in GI with Derm momentum building; TREMFYA perception improves significantly post-FRM interaction (~2/3 of HCPs)
  • Strong PSP intent; high-value GI topics (IODP, HUB, copay, billing) resonate

Watch items:

  • ~55-60% of HCPs report no motivation to act post-interaction — the "so what" gap
  • Perception impact lower in Derm and Rheum vs GI; Rheum problem-solving rated significantly lower vs AbbVie

Recs from latest readout:

  • Shift from reactive to proactive engagement; increase Derm touchpoints
  • Reinforce high-value GI topics; address Rheum problem-solving gap head-on before AbbVie widens lead

SharePoint: [link]

GASTRO MSL (Immunology)

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • J&J leads the market: 88% reach, 36% SOV — dominant engagement position
  • Interactions are proactive, in-person, with extensive visual aid use; slight lead over AbbVie on overall call quality

Watch items:

  • Guselkumab slightly lags risankizumab in SDP (Scientific Data Platform) effectiveness despite strong post-interaction knowledge/perception improvement
  • Depth of clinical knowledge remains an area for enhancement

Recs from latest readout:

  • Enhance clinical knowledge depth and equip MSLs with better tools to improve perceptions
  • Sustain proactive, in-person engagement model; maintain visual aid discipline

SharePoint: [link]

IMM KAM-HS

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • Leading market presence: superior reach, frequency, and SOV
  • Proactively reaching out, in-person, with extensive visual aid use

Watch items:

  • Performance only tracks (does not lead) competitors among IDN-based stakeholders
  • Non-IDN coverage and formulary addition discussions are gaps

Recs from latest readout:

  • Increase non-IDN focus to capture underserved accounts
  • Strengthen problem-solving and formulary addition capabilities; increase pre-approval engagement content

SharePoint: [link]

IMM ABS

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • J&J co-leads SOV with AbbVie; strong proactive engagement model
  • ABS team maintains at-par performance vs competitors across most attributes

Watch items:

  • "At par" is not differentiated — no clear performance advantage despite SOV co-leadership
  • Formulary/EHR additions and supply chain/infusion coordination topics are underdeveloped

Recs from latest readout:

  • Increase visit frequency to translate SOV parity into a lead
  • Focus on formulary/EHR additions and strengthen supply chain and infusion coordination topics

SharePoint: [link]

IMM KAM-MD

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • J&J has leading 50% SOV; ~95% of stakeholders have met a J&J KAM-MD — near-total coverage
  • Strong market presence foundation to build on

Watch items:

  • Frequency needs to increase despite high coverage — presence does not equal depth
  • Product/clinical knowledge tailoring and supply chain, insurance, and patient access content need strengthening

Recs from latest readout:

  • Increase frequency to match the coverage advantage
  • Enhance clinical knowledge tailoring; focus content on supply chain, insurance, and patient access

SharePoint: [link]

Neuroscience

See SPRAVATO PET above (listed under Oncology PET for structural consistency with the tracker type, but this is a Neuroscience brand).

Social Listening

RYBREVANT Social Listening

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2025 Executive Summary | Current wave: Q4 2025

What's working:

  • Growing RYBREVANT online discussion volume, albeit smaller than Tagrisso
  • RYBREVANT mentions driven by news and events — opportunity to shape narrative around data milestones

Watch items:

  • Tagrisso dominates online conversation, driven by direct patient/caregiver experience — more organic and sticky
  • Patient misconceptions persist: generalizing Keytruda success to EGFR, believing RYBREVANT is only for exon-20

Recs from latest readout:

  • Position RYBREVANT as a 1L option; highlight Faspro (SubQ) benefits; use COCOON/SKIPPirr data
  • Develop mutation testing guides and caregiver resources to address misconceptions

SharePoint: [link]

MEE (New 2026)

MEE Tracking (All TAs)

Status: New — In Recruitment | Latest: Skeleton deck master finalized Mar 31, 2026 | Current wave: Q1 2026 (first wave)

What's working:

  • Standardized MSL effectiveness tracking launched across ALL J&J TAs — the first unified framework
  • Q1 2026 kick-offs completed for Lung, GU, Heme, Neuroscience, and Immunology

Watch items:

  • Currently in recruitment phase; weekly recruitment and leadership updates active as of Apr 7
  • First wave data not yet available — establishing baselines will be critical

Recs from latest readout:

  • Monitor recruitment progress weekly; flag any TA-specific recruitment challenges early
  • Prepare stakeholders for baseline-setting expectations (no QoQ comparisons possible in Wave 1)

SharePoint: [link]

Business Development

BD / Growth Initiatives

Status: Active | Latest: TREMFYA GI Global Proposal (March 2026) | Current phase: Proposal + Planning

Active BD items:

  • TREMFYA GI Global Proposal: $568K total scope (SFE + MEE + Rx Integration) — submitted March 2026
  • 2026 BD Resources & Process: Documented and distributed to team
  • 2026 IMM PET Takeover: Planning document in circulation
  • ZoomRx J&J Workshop 2026: Planned

Watch items:

  • TREMFYA global proposal is the single largest expansion opportunity; track decision timeline closely
  • IMM PET Takeover represents a competitive displacement — requires careful positioning

SharePoint: [link]

Cross-Workstream Themes

1. Presence Without Depth: The SOV-to-Impact Gap

J&J leads or co-leads SOV in nearly every workstream — ERLEADA (ONCs and UROs), GU MSL, GASTRO MSL, KAM, IMM KAM-HS, IMM KAM-MD, IMM ABS, and MM OCE. Yet in several of these, performance metrics (call quality, clinical knowledge, problem-solving) only match or trail competitors. The pattern is clearest in GU MSL (trails on enhancing understanding), IMM ABS (at par despite SOV co-lead), IMM FRM (55-60% report no motivation to act), and ERLEADA ONCs (call quality declined). Implication for the Principal: Raw presence is not the differentiator J&J thinks it is. The strategic narrative across workstreams should push toward converting presence into impact through clinical depth, tailored content, and problem-solving.

2. Access and Economic Discussions Are Universally Underdeveloped

Access, reimbursement, and economic topics surface as gaps in ERLEADA UROs (low FRM follow-up on access), KAM (reimbursement discussion frequency decreased), TECVAYLI PHDMs (cost is top challenge), IMM KAM-HS (formulary additions), IMM ABS (formulary/EHR), and IMM KAM-MD (insurance and patient access). This is not a single-brand problem — it is a systemic gap in how J&J field teams are equipped and incentivized to handle economic conversations. Implication for the Principal: Flag this as a cross-TA theme to J&J leadership. Propose a dedicated access/economic discussion module that could be tracked across multiple workstreams simultaneously.

3. Competitor Momentum Is Segment-Specific and Accelerating

Nubeqa SOV hit an all-time high in prostate ONCs. Vraylar leads SPRAVATO in neuroscience SOV. AbbVie's risankizumab slightly outperforms guselkumab on SDP effectiveness. Elrexfio is improving on impact metrics against TECVAYLI. These are not broad competitive shifts — they are targeted gains in specific segments (RWE Targets, Utility Players, IDN stakeholders, specific HCP personas). Implication for the Principal: Ensure each workstream's ES and recs address competitor momentum at the segment level, not just overall. Help J&J see which segments are most vulnerable to erosion.

4. The MEE Expansion Creates a Unifying Measurement Framework

With MEE tracking now launching across Lung, GU, Heme, Neuroscience, and Immunology, there is a first-ever opportunity to compare MSL effectiveness across TAs using a standardized methodology. This aligns with the existing MSL trackers (GU MSL, GASTRO MSL) but extends coverage and creates a common language. Implication for the Principal: Position the MEE roll-out as a strategic platform, not just another tracker. Use it to drive cross-TA benchmarking conversations that elevate ZoomRx's strategic value beyond brand-by-brand reporting.

Immediate Priorities (First 2 Weeks)

  1. Read every Q4 2025 ES deck. There are 11+ readout decks from the most recent wave. Read the executive summaries and recommendation slides for each. Your credibility depends on knowing the current state of every workstream before your first stakeholder meeting.

  2. Understand the SPRAVATO situation. This is the workstream most at risk — steep LTIP and perception decline driven by Utility Players. Expect this to come up in conversations with the Neuroscience team. Know the segment-level story and the messaging recs.

  3. Get briefed on MEE recruitment status. MEE is the highest-visibility new initiative. Weekly updates are active. Ask the team lead for the latest recruitment tracker and any TA-specific challenges. You will be asked about this.

  4. Track the TREMFYA global proposal decision. At $568K, this is the largest single growth opportunity on the account. Understand the scope (SFE + MEE + Rx Integration), the decision-maker, and the timeline.

  5. Map the cross-workstream themes to upcoming stakeholder meetings. The four themes above (SOV-to-impact gap, access discussions, competitor momentum, MEE unification) are the kind of cross-cutting insights that differentiate a Principal from a project manager. Identify which stakeholder meetings in the next 2 weeks are best suited to surface each theme.

  6. Review the ERLEADA ONC vs URO divergence. Same brand, opposite performance trajectories in Q4 2025 (ONCs declining, UROs improving). This is a ready-made insight for Ryan Catania (Director, Prostate) — ask what is driving the specialist-specific divergence and whether the field teams are aware of it.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

Governing Thought
The J&J account has 14 active workstreams generating regular deliverables across Oncology, Immunology, Neuroscience, and cross-brand programs. The account is in growth mode: MEE (MSL Effectiveness Evaluation) expansion across all TAs kicked off in Q1 2026, a TREMFYA global proposal worth $568K is in motion, and established trackers are performing consistently. Three performance themes cut across nearly every workstream: (1) J&J leads in market presence and SOV across most workstreams, giving the field force a structural advantage; (2) execution gaps persist in clinical depth, access discussions, and problem-solving attributes, meaning presence alone is not converting to maximum impact; and (3) emerging competitor momentum — Nubeqa in prostate, Vraylar in neuroscience, AbbVie in immunology — is narrowing or erasing J&J's leads in specific segments. A Principal's job is to connect these dots across workstreams so J&J stakeholders see the system-level story, not just brand-by-brand scorecards.

Cross-Workstream Themes
1. Presence Without Depth: The SOV-to-Impact Gap
J&J leads or co-leads SOV in nearly every workstream — ERLEADA (ONCs and UROs), GU MSL, GASTRO MSL, KAM, IMM KAM-HS, IMM KAM-MD, IMM ABS, and MM OCE. Yet in several of these, performance metrics (call quality, clinical knowledge, problem-solving) only match or trail competitors. The pattern is clearest in GU MSL (trails on enhancing understanding), IMM ABS (at par despite SOV co-lead), IMM FRM (55-60% report no motivation to act), and ERLEADA ONCs (call quality declined). Implication for the Principal: Raw presence is not the differentiator J&J thinks it is. The strategic narrative across workstreams should push toward converting presence into impact through clinical depth, tailored content, and problem-solving.
2. Access and Economic Discussions Are Universally Underdeveloped
Access, reimbursement, and economic topics surface as gaps in ERLEADA UROs (low FRM follow-up on access), KAM (reimbursement discussion frequency decreased), TECVAYLI PHDMs (cost is top challenge), IMM KAM-HS (formulary additions), IMM ABS (formulary/EHR), and IMM KAM-MD (insurance and patient access). This is not a single-brand problem — it is a systemic gap in how J&J field teams are equipped and incentivized to handle economic conversations. Implication for the Principal: Flag this as a cross-TA theme to J&J leadership. Propose a dedicated access/economic discussion module that could be tracked across multiple workstreams simultaneously.
3. Competitor Momentum Is Segment-Specific and Accelerating
Nubeqa SOV hit an all-time high in prostate ONCs. Vraylar leads SPRAVATO in neuroscience SOV. AbbVie's risankizumab slightly outperforms guselkumab on SDP effectiveness. Elrexfio is improving on impact metrics against TECVAYLI. These are not broad competitive shifts — they are targeted gains in specific segments (RWE Targets, Utility Players, IDN stakeholders, specific HCP personas). Implication for the Principal: Ensure each workstream's ES and recs address competitor momentum at the segment level, not just overall. Help J&J see which segments are most vulnerable to erosion.
4. The MEE Expansion Creates a Unifying Measurement Framework
With MEE tracking now launching across Lung, GU, Heme, Neuroscience, and Immunology, there is a first-ever opportunity to compare MSL effectiveness across TAs using a standardized methodology. This aligns with the existing MSL trackers (GU MSL, GASTRO MSL) but extends coverage and creates a common language. Implication for the Principal: Position the MEE roll-out as a strategic platform, not just another tracker. Use it to drive cross-TA benchmarking conversations that elevate ZoomRx's strategic value beyond brand-by-brand reporting.
